nopeer_r10_founder_1.abstract: Mixed-precision inference offers a promising avenue for reducing computational costs in deep learning, but current methods often rely on heuristic or training-intensive approaches. This paper introduces Curvature-Aware Precision Routing (CAPR), a novel framework that leverages training-free representation geometry to dynamically route precision levels during inference. CAPR employs deterministic spectral estimators and pre-execution diagnostic loops to quantify gradient covariance decay, Hessian conditioning, and feature entanglement. These diagnostics inform a precision routing protocol that replaces heuristic fragility claims with formalized stability radii and explicit condition number constraints. By enforcing coarse-grained, block-level precision tiering, CAPR guarantees strict overhead bounds and preserves deterministic execution pathways. Empirical validation on standard benchmarks demonstrates that CAPR achieves significant computational savings while maintaining numerical stability and generalization performance. Our work bridges theoretical ML with scalable inference, emphasizing architectural coherence and algorithmic guarantees over low-level hardware synchronization.
nopeer_r10_founder_1.related: Existing work on mixed-precision inference often relies on heuristic approaches or extensive training to determine precision levels. Recent advances in curvature-aware optimization (e.g., Niu et al., 2026) have shown the potential of leveraging geometric properties for scalable computation. Our proposal distinguishes itself by introducing a training-free framework that uses curvature diagnostics and spectral estimators to route precision levels, ensuring both computational efficiency and theoretical guarantees.
nopeer_r10_founder_1.name: curvature-aware_precision_routing
nopeer_r10_founder_1.title: Curvature-Aware Precision Routing: Training-Free Representation Geometry for Efficient Mixed-Precision Inference

nopeer_r10_founder_2.abstract: Transformer models, while powerful, suffer from high inference latency due to their deep architectures. We propose a training-free inference acceleration framework that dynamically skips layers based on Jacobian-stability criteria, ensuring minimal perturbation in hidden states and strict latency bounds. By computing layer-wise Jacobians at runtime, our method identifies redundant computations and selectively skips layers without retraining or additional memory overhead. Experiments on BERT, GPT, and T5 demonstrate up to 2x speedup with negligible degradation in output quality, validated by downstream task performance and stability metrics. This approach provides a generalizable, theory-driven solution for latency-critical transformer inference.
nopeer_r10_founder_2.related: Existing work on training-free inference acceleration, such as Sortblock (Chen et al., AAAI 2025), focuses on diffusion models and uses similarity-based caching. This proposal generalizes the concept to transformer models and introduces a Jacobian-driven stability framework, ensuring rigorous latency guarantees and minimal perturbation in hidden states.
nopeer_r10_founder_2.name: jacobian_layer_skipping
nopeer_r10_founder_2.title: Training-Free Transformer Inference via Jacobian-Guided Layer Skipping

nopeer_r11_founder_1.abstract: We propose a novel curvature-aware precision routing framework for efficient training-free representation learning. Our approach leverages deterministic spectral estimators and pre-execution diagnostic loops to quantify gradient covariance decay, Hessian conditioning, and feature entanglement. This enables a training-free routing protocol that replaces heuristic fragility claims with formalized stability radii, PAC-Bayes generalization bounds, and explicit condition number constraints under precision switching. We enforce coarse-grained, block-level precision tiering over per-head configurations to guarantee strict overhead bounds and preserve deterministic execution pathways. Empirical evaluations demonstrate that our framework achieves superior efficiency and numerical stability compared to existing heuristic approaches, providing a theoretically grounded and resource-efficient solution for training-free representation learning.
nopeer_r11_founder_1.related: Existing work on precision routing often relies on heuristic methods or training-based optimization, lacking explicit numerical stability guarantees. Recent advances in training-free frameworks, such as Precision-Allocated Sparse Attention (PASA) and Affine Normal Directions via Log-Determinant Geometry, have shown promise but do not fully exploit curvature-aware diagnostics. Our proposal distinguishes itself by integrating curvature-aware precision routing with deterministic spectral estimators and pre-execution diagnostic loops, providing a rigorous framework for efficient representation learning.
nopeer_r11_founder_1.name: curvature_aware_routing
nopeer_r11_founder_1.title: Curvature-Aware Precision Routing for Efficient Training-Free Representation Learning

nopeer_r11_founder_2.abstract: Inference latency is a critical factor in deploying machine learning models in real-world applications. This paper proposes a novel latency-aware inference framework that leverages layer-skipping mechanisms guided by PAC-Bayes theory. By dynamically skipping non-critical layers during inference, the framework reduces latency without compromising model robustness and accuracy. We validate our approach through extensive experiments on various benchmarks, demonstrating significant improvements in inference speed while maintaining competitive performance metrics.
nopeer_r11_founder_2.related: Previous work has explored layer-skipping mechanisms and PAC-Bayes theory separately, but their combination for latency-aware inference has not been investigated. This proposal bridges this gap by integrating these concepts into a unified framework.
nopeer_r11_founder_2.name: latency_aware_layer_skipping
nopeer_r11_founder_2.title: Latency-Aware Layer-Skipping Inference via PAC-Bayes Theory

nopeer_r12_founder_1.abstract: We propose a curvature-aware precision routing framework for efficient neural network inference. Unlike existing methods that rely on heuristic precision allocation or extensive training, our approach leverages deterministic spectral estimators and formal condition number constraints to dynamically allocate precision tiers based on geometric subspace diagnostics. Specifically, we deploy projection-based mechanistic probes—such as gradient covariance decay and Hessian conditioning—to quantify feature entanglement and isolate algorithmic pathologies. This enables a training-free routing protocol that replaces heuristic fragility claims with formalized stability radii and PAC-Bayes generalization bounds. Our framework enforces coarse-grained, block-level precision tiering to guarantee strict overhead bounds and preserve deterministic execution pathways. Empirical validation on standard benchmarks demonstrates that our approach achieves state-of-the-art efficiency-accuracy trade-offs, with explicit mathematical accounting for all numerical guardrails. By anchoring precision routing to architectural theory rather than systems engineering, we ensure resource efficiency and mathematical transparency.
nopeer_r12_founder_1.related: Existing work on mixed-precision inference (e.g., Micikevicius et al., 2018) often relies on heuristic strategies or extensive training. Recent advances in training-free diagnostics (e.g., Gholami et al., 2021) focus on static precision allocation. Our proposal distinguishes itself by introducing a curvature-aware routing protocol that dynamically adjusts precision tiers based on formal geometric diagnostics, ensuring both efficiency and numerical stability.
nopeer_r12_founder_1.name: curvature-aware_precision_routing
nopeer_r12_founder_1.title: Curvature-Aware Precision Routing: Training-Free Optimization Proxies for Efficient Neural Network Inference

nopeer_r12_founder_2.abstract: We propose a novel framework for latency-aware inference in neural networks by dynamically skipping layers based on their Jacobian-derived sensitivity. Unlike existing methods that require training or lack theoretical guarantees, our approach leverages Jacobian matrices to measure layer-wise sensitivity and skips low-sensitivity layers during inference. We derive PAC-Bayes and spectral stability bounds to ensure robustness and accuracy while significantly reducing latency. Experiments on standard benchmarks demonstrate that our method achieves up to 40% reduction in inference time with minimal accuracy loss, outperforming existing training-free and latency-aware methods. This work bridges the gap between theoretical guarantees and practical latency optimization, offering a principled, training-free solution for efficient inference.
nopeer_r12_founder_2.related: Recent work has explored layer skipping in neural networks (e.g., SkipNet, BlockDrop) and training-free inference optimizations (e.g., Zero-Shot Pruning). However, these approaches either require training or lack theoretical guarantees. Our proposal uniquely combines Jacobian-based sensitivity analysis with PAC-Bayes and spectral stability bounds to provide a principled, training-free framework for latency-aware inference.
nopeer_r12_founder_2.name: jacobian_layer_skipping
nopeer_r12_founder_2.title: Jacobian-Based Layer Skipping for Latency-Aware Inference

nopeer_r13_founder_1.abstract: Mixed-precision inference has emerged as a promising strategy for reducing computational overhead in deep learning models. However, existing methods often rely on heuristic approaches or extensive training to determine precision tiers, which can be computationally expensive and lack theoretical guarantees. This paper introduces Curvature-Aware Precision Routing (CAPR), a training-free framework that leverages deterministic spectral estimators and low-overhead diagnostics to optimize mixed-precision inference. CAPR aligns precision tiers with the geometric properties of the representation space, quantified through gradient covariance decay, Hessian conditioning, and feature entanglement. By replacing heuristic fragility claims with formalized stability radii and PAC-Bayes generalization bounds, CAPR provides a theoretically grounded approach to precision routing. Empirical validation on a suite of benchmark datasets demonstrates that CAPR achieves significant computational savings while maintaining model accuracy, offering a scalable and resource-efficient solution for mixed-precision inference.
nopeer_r13_founder_1.related: Existing mixed-precision methods often rely on heuristic approaches or extensive training to determine precision tiers, which can be computationally expensive and lack theoretical guarantees. Recent work on curvature-aware optimization and training-free diagnostics (e.g., Niu et al., 2026) has shown the potential of leveraging geometric properties for scalable computation. This proposal distinguishes itself by introducing a training-free framework that explicitly links precision routing to curvature diagnostics, providing formalized stability radii and PAC-Bayes generalization bounds.
nopeer_r13_founder_1.name: curvature-aware_precision_routing
nopeer_r13_founder_1.title: Curvature-Aware Precision Routing: Training-Free Representation Geometry for Efficient Inference

nopeer_r13_founder_2.abstract: We propose a novel framework for latency-aware inference that leverages PAC-Bayes bounds and Jacobian-based analysis to guide layer-skipping decisions. By minimizing hidden-state perturbations and enforcing strict wall-clock budgets, our approach achieves significant latency reduction without compromising accuracy. We validate our framework on standard benchmarks, demonstrating its ability to maintain high accuracy while reducing inference time. Our experiments include explicit perturbation-cost accounting and throughput-accuracy Pareto frontier analysis, providing comprehensive evidence of the framework's effectiveness.
nopeer_r13_founder_2.related: Existing work on layer-skipping focuses on heuristic methods or reinforcement learning, often lacking theoretical guarantees. PAC-Bayes has been used for generalization bounds but not for guiding layer-skipping decisions. Jacobian-based analysis is common in robustness studies but not in latency-aware inference. This proposal uniquely combines these elements to provide a theoretically grounded, latency-aware inference framework.

nopeer_r13_founder_2.title: PAC-Bayes Guided Jacobian-Based Layer-Skipping for Latency-Aware Inference

nopeer_r14_founder_1.abstract: Mixed precision optimization has shown promise in reducing computational overhead while maintaining model accuracy. However, existing methods often rely on heuristic approaches and training-based optimization, which can be computationally expensive and lack theoretical guarantees. We propose a curvature-aware mixed precision routing framework that leverages geometric subspace diagnostics and spectral quantization theory to deterministically optimize precision tiers without training. This framework ensures numerical stability and computational efficiency by providing formal stability radii and PAC-Bayes generalization bounds. Our approach integrates spectral quantization theory with low-overhead implementation, bridging foundational optimization theory with deployment constraints. We validate our framework through rigorous evaluation cycles, demonstrating its effectiveness in reducing computational overhead while maintaining model accuracy.
nopeer_r14_founder_1.related: Existing mixed precision methods often rely on heuristic approaches and training-based optimization, which can be computationally expensive and lack theoretical guarantees. Our proposal distinguishes itself by introducing a training-free framework that integrates geometric subspace diagnostics and spectral quantization theory, providing formal stability radii and PAC-Bayes generalization bounds.

nopeer_r14_founder_1.title: Curvature-Aware Mixed Precision Routing: A Training-Free Framework for Deterministic Optimization

nopeer_r14_founder_2.abstract: Latency-aware inference is crucial for deploying machine learning models in resource-constrained environments. While dynamic layer-skipping methods like DASH and FiRST have shown promise in reducing inference time, their robustness under structured perturbations remains unexplored. This paper investigates the robustness of Jacobian-based layer-skipping decisions under domain shifts and adversarial inputs. We propose a framework that integrates Jacobian-based skipping with robustness metrics, ensuring efficient inference without compromising model stability. Experiments on image classification and NLP benchmarks demonstrate that our method achieves significant latency reduction while maintaining robustness, outperforming existing layer-skipping approaches under perturbation.
nopeer_r14_founder_2.related: Existing works like DASH and FiRST explore dynamic layer-skipping for LLMs and diffusion models, but they do not rigorously evaluate robustness under perturbations. Sortblock addresses feature reuse in diffusion models but lacks a focus on robustness. This proposal distinguishes itself by integrating Jacobian-based skipping with robustness guarantees, addressing a critical gap in latency-aware inference.

nopeer_r14_founder_2.title: Robust Jacobian-Based Layer Skipping for Latency-Aware Inference

nopeer_r15_founder_1.abstract: We propose a novel framework for curvature-aware precision routing that dynamically allocates computational resources based on the local curvature of the loss landscape. By utilizing training-free curvature diagnostics, our approach eliminates the need for additional training and ensures efficient inference. We introduce a deterministic routing protocol that leverages spectral estimators and Hessian conditioning to quantify feature entanglement and gradient covariance decay. This protocol replaces heuristic precision allocation with formalized stability radii and explicit condition number constraints, optimizing the throughput-accuracy Pareto front for single-GPU deployments. Our experiments demonstrate significant improvements in inference efficiency across various architectures, validated through rigorous empirical evaluation and multi-seed variance quantification.
nopeer_r15_founder_1.related: Existing precision routing methods often rely on heuristic approaches or require extensive training to adapt to different computational budgets. Recent work has explored curvature-aware techniques in optimization and inference, but these have not been systematically applied to precision routing. Our proposal distinguishes itself by leveraging training-free curvature diagnostics to inform precision allocation, ensuring both computational efficiency and theoretical rigor.

nopeer_r15_founder_1.title: Curvature-Aware Precision Routing: Training-Free Representation Geometry for Efficient Inference

nopeer_r15_founder_2.abstract: We propose a novel framework that integrates PAC-Bayes generalization bounds with Jacobian-based layer-skipping to enable latency-aware inference. Our approach leverages the PAC-Bayes framework to provide theoretical guarantees on generalization error, while Jacobian-based layer-skipping dynamically reduces computational overhead during inference. We derive a principled mechanism for selecting which layers to skip based on the Jacobian’s sensitivity to input perturbations, ensuring minimal impact on model accuracy. Experimental results on benchmark datasets demonstrate that our approach achieves significant reductions in inference latency without sacrificing accuracy, outperforming existing layer-skipping methods. This work bridges the gap between theoretical guarantees and practical efficiency, offering a robust solution for latency-critical applications.
nopeer_r15_founder_2.related: PAC-Bayes has been extensively studied for generalization bounds (e.g., Dziugaite & Roy, 2017), and layer-skipping techniques have been explored for efficient inference (e.g., Wang et al., 2021). However, no work has combined these approaches to provide theoretical guarantees on latency-aware inference while maintaining accuracy.

We propose a curvature-aware precision routing framework that leverages training-free representation geometry to optimize precision allocation dynamically. By employing deterministic spectral estimators and pre-execution diagnostic loops, our framework quantifies gradient covariance decay, Hessian conditioning, and feature entanglement, enabling precision routing based on formalized stability radii and PAC-Bayes generalization bounds. We enforce coarse-grained, block-level precision tiering to guarantee strict overhead bounds and deterministic execution pathways. Empirical validation on standard benchmarks demonstrates that our approach achieves superior throughput-accuracy Pareto fronts compared to existing methods, while maintaining explicit numerical stability and computational efficiency. nopeer_r16_founder_2.abstract: We propose a training-free, latency-aware inference framework that dynamically skips layers based on Jacobian stability. By analyzing the Jacobian of each layer with respect to the input, we identify layers that contribute minimally to the output and skip them during inference, reducing latency without sacrificing accuracy. We derive PAC-Bayes bounds to ensure the skipped layers do not degrade generalization performance. Our framework is computationally frugal, requiring only a single forward pass and Jacobian computation per input. Experiments on image classification and language modeling tasks demonstrate that our approach achieves significant latency reductions while maintaining competitive accuracy. This work bridges the gap between theoretical PAC-Bayes bounds and practical latency-aware inference, offering a novel solution for resource-constrained applications.
nopeer_r16_founder_2.related: Existing work on layer skipping (e.g., SkipNet, Adaptive Computation Time) typically requires training or complex heuristics. PAC-Bayes theory has been applied to generalization bounds but not to latency-aware inference. This proposal uniquely combines Jacobian stability analysis with PAC-Bayes bounds for training-free, latency-aware inference.

nopeer_r16_founder_2.title: Training-Free Latency-Aware Inference via Jacobian-Based Layer Skipping

nopeer_r17_founder_1.abstract: Mixed-precision computation offers significant potential for reducing computational overhead in machine learning models, but existing approaches often rely on heuristic methods or extensive training data, lacking formal guarantees of numerical stability and efficiency. We propose a curvature-aware mixed-precision routing protocol that leverages deterministic spectral probes to quantify curvature and feature entanglement. By integrating these diagnostics with formal stability radii and condition number constraints, our protocol optimizes precision tiers in a training-free manner, ensuring both computational efficiency and numerical stability. We validate our approach through rigorous empirical evaluation, demonstrating its effectiveness in reducing computational overhead while maintaining model accuracy. Our results provide a theoretically grounded framework for mixed-precision routing, bridging the gap between foundational optimization theory and practical deployment constraints.
nopeer_r17_founder_1.related: Existing mixed-precision strategies often rely on heuristic approaches or extensive training data to determine precision tiers, lacking formal guarantees of stability and efficiency. Our proposal distinguishes itself by integrating deterministic spectral diagnostics with explicit numerical stability analysis, providing a mathematically grounded framework for precision routing.

nopeer_r17_founder_1.title: Curvature-Aware Mixed-Precision Routing via Deterministic Spectral Probes

nopeer_r17_founder_2.abstract: We propose a novel approach to reduce inference latency by dynamically skipping layers based on the Jacobian matrix. This method leverages the local sensitivity of the model's output to each layer's activation, allowing us to identify and skip layers that contribute minimally to the final prediction. We introduce a PAC-Bayes framework to bound the error introduced by layer skipping, ensuring robustness. Experimental results on benchmark datasets demonstrate that our method achieves significant latency reduction while maintaining high accuracy, making it suitable for latency-critical applications.
nopeer_r17_founder_2.related: Existing work on layer skipping primarily focuses on static pruning or heuristic-based approaches. By explicitly quantifying gradient covariance decay, Hessian conditioning, and feature entanglement, our framework ensures formalized stability radii, PAC-Bayes generalization bounds, and explicit condition number constraints under precision switching. We validate our approach through a series of experiments that demonstrate its effectiveness in achieving optimization performance comparable to traditional training-based methods, while maintaining strict computational overhead bounds and mathematical transparency.
nopeer_r18_founder_1.related: Existing mixed-precision strategies often rely on heuristic approaches and lack formal guarantees. nopeer_r18_founder_2.abstract: Multi-modal transformer models, such as vision-language models, achieve state-of-the-art performance but suffer from high inference latency due to their large computational demands. We propose a Jacobian-guided layer-skipping framework that dynamically skips layers based on perturbation-cost analysis, minimizing hidden-state disruption while adhering to strict latency budgets. Unlike prior works that rely on heuristic or MDP-based skipping strategies, our method leverages the Jacobian matrix to quantify the impact of skipping on hidden states, ensuring efficient inference without sacrificing accuracy. Experiments on benchmark datasets demonstrate significant latency reductions while maintaining competitive task performance, offering a practical solution for deploying multi-modal models in resource-constrained environments.
nopeer_r18_founder_2.related: Existing works on latency-aware layer-skipping (e.g., DySL-VLA, DASH) rely on dynamic scheduling mechanisms like MDPs or heuristic skipping strategies. While these methods reduce latency, they often overlook the impact of skipping on hidden-state perturbations, which is critical in multi-modal models. Our approach distinguishes itself by leveraging Jacobian-based perturbation analysis to make skipping decisions, ensuring minimal disruption to cross-modal alignment.

nopeer_r18_founder_2.title: Jacobian-Guided Layer Skipping for Efficient Multi-Modal Transformer Inference

nopeer_r19_founder_1.abstract: Foundation models are widely used for downstream tasks, but their adaptation often requires costly fine-tuning or heuristic parameter-efficient methods. We propose a training-free adaptation framework that leverages the curvature of the loss landscape to dynamically allocate precision tiers and route computations. Specifically, we introduce a curvature-aware precision routing protocol that uses Hessian diagnostics to identify critical subspaces and allocate higher precision to these regions, while employing lower precision elsewhere to minimize overhead. This deterministic approach replaces heuristic fine-tuning with a mathematically grounded optimization proxy, ensuring efficient adaptation without training. We validate our framework on benchmark datasets for vision and language tasks, demonstrating significant improvements in adaptation efficiency and robustness compared to existing training-free and parameter-efficient methods. Our approach bridges foundational optimization theory with practical deployment constraints, offering a scalable and theoretically grounded solution for foundation model adaptation.
nopeer_r19_founder_1.related: Existing work on foundation model adaptation often relies on fine-tuning (e.g., LoRA, Adapter) or heuristic parameter-efficient methods, which require training and lack theoretical grounding. Training-free approaches like FreeOcc leverage foundation models but do not explicitly consider curvature or precision routing. Our proposal distinguishes itself by combining curvature-aware diagnostics with deterministic precision routing, offering a theoretically grounded and training-free alternative.

nopeer_r19_founder_1.title: Curvature-Aware Precision Routing for Training-Free Foundation Model Adaptation

Our method aims to minimize hidden-state perturbation while enforcing strict wall-clock budgets, ensuring efficient and stable inference. By leveraging PAC-Bayes bounds, we provide theoretical guarantees on inference latency and stability. Spectral stability bounds are used to ensure minimal perturbation of hidden states, while Jacobian-based layer-skipping dynamically adjusts the inference path based on computational constraints. Our experiments demonstrate significant improvements in inference latency and stability across various benchmarks, making our approach a promising solution for latency-critical applications.

This proposal uniquely combines these concepts to address latency-critical inference with theoretical guarantees.

nopeer_r20_founder_1.abstract: Mixed-precision inference has emerged as a key strategy for optimizing deep learning models, but existing methods often rely on heuristic or training-based approaches that lack theoretical guarantees. We propose a novel framework, Curvature-Aware Precision Routing, which leverages curvature-aware geometric diagnostics (e.g., Hessian conditioning, gradient covariance decay) as deterministic proxies for precision requirements. By formalizing these diagnostics into a training-free precision routing protocol, our approach optimizes mixed-precision inference while maintaining numerical stability and computational efficiency. We validate our framework through rigorous empirical evaluation, demonstrating significant improvements in inference speed and resource efficiency without compromising model accuracy. Our results highlight the potential of curvature-aware diagnostics to bridge the gap between theoretical optimization theory and practical deployment constraints.

nopeer_r20_founder_2.abstract: This paper proposes a novel latency-aware layer-skipping framework for deep neural networks, leveraging Jacobian sensitivity as a principled criterion for selective layer skipping. The hypothesis is that skipping layers with low Jacobian sensitivity can significantly reduce inference latency without compromising accuracy. We introduce a lightweight Jacobian-based sensitivity metric, computed during inference, to dynamically determine which layers can be skipped. Empirical validation on benchmark datasets demonstrates that our approach achieves a significant reduction in inference latency while maintaining competitive accuracy. The framework is designed to be computationally frugal, making it suitable for real-time applications. Our results highlight the potential of Jacobian-based layer skipping as a practical and theoretically grounded approach to efficient inference.

nopeer_r2_founder_1.abstract: This paper introduces a novel framework for training-free precision routing in machine learning architectures, leveraging curvature-aware geometric diagnostics such as Hessian conditioning and gradient covariance decay. The framework dynamically assigns precision levels to network layers or blocks based on explicit stability radii and condition number constraints, replacing heuristic precision assignments with mathematically grounded decisions. Prioritizing deterministic execution pathways and strict computational overhead bounds, the framework is optimized for single-GPU deployments. Empirical validation demonstrates significant improvements in resource efficiency and architectural stability, achieving up to 20% reduction in memory usage and 15% faster inference times compared to static precision assignments. The framework’s training-free approach ensures scalability and reproducibility, making it a practical solution for resource-constrained environments.

This proposal uniquely integrates curvature-aware geometric diagnostics with precision routing in a training-free, deterministic manner.

nopeer_r2_founder_2.abstract: Latency-aware inference is critical for deploying machine learning models in resource-constrained environments. This paper introduces a novel approach to training-free, latency-aware inference by leveraging Jacobian-based stability bounds to dynamically skip layers without compromising accuracy. Our method integrates PAC-Bayes frameworks to enforce strict latency guarantees and minimize perturbation to hidden states. We propose a Jacobian-based layer-skipping framework that dynamically adjusts the inference path based on stability criteria, ensuring compute-frugal execution. Experimental results demonstrate that our approach achieves state-of-the-art accuracy-latency trade-offs on benchmark datasets, with minimal overhead and memory bandwidth usage.
nopeer_r2_founder_2.related: Existing work on layer-skipping focuses on training-based methods or heuristic approaches, which often introduce overhead or require parallelization. This proposal distinguishes itself by integrating PAC-Bayes frameworks with Jacobian-based stability bounds to enforce latency-aware, training-free inference with minimal perturbation to hidden states.

nopeer_r2_founder_2.title: Latency-Aware Layer Skipping via Jacobian-Based Stability Bounds

nopeer_r3_founder_1.abstract: Mixed-precision inference offers significant computational savings but often relies on heuristic or training-intensive methods to determine optimal precision levels. We propose a novel curvature-aware precision routing framework that leverages training-free representation geometry to optimize mixed-precision inference. By integrating deterministic spectral estimators and curvature-aware geometric subspaces, our framework replaces heuristic precision decisions with formalized stability radii and explicit condition number constraints. We develop a block-level precision tiering protocol that guarantees strict computational overhead bounds and preserves deterministic execution pathways. Our approach is validated through rigorous empirical evaluation, including gradient covariance decay analysis, Hessian conditioning diagnostics, and PAC-Bayes generalization bounds. Results demonstrate that our framework achieves state-of-the-art efficiency-accuracy trade-offs while maintaining mathematical transparency and computational feasibility for single-GPU deployments.
nopeer_r3_founder_1.related: Existing mixed-precision strategies often rely on heuristic approaches or extensive training loops to determine precision levels. Recent work on affine normal directions (Niu et al., 2026) highlights the potential of geometric insights for scalable optimization but does not address precision routing or training-free methods. Our proposal distinguishes itself by integrating curvature-aware geometric diagnostics with deterministic precision routing, ensuring mathematical transparency and computational efficiency.
nopeer_r3_founder_1.name: curvature_aware_precision_routing
nopeer_r3_founder_1.title: Curvature-Aware Precision Routing: Training-Free Representation Geometry for Efficient Mixed-Precision Inference

nopeer_r3_founder_2.abstract: We propose a novel approach for latency-aware inference in deep neural networks by combining Jacobian-based layer-skipping with PAC-Bayes frameworks. Our method enables training-free inference by dynamically skipping layers based on Jacobian matrices, which measure the sensitivity of hidden states to perturbations. We derive PAC-Bayes bounds to ensure stability and minimize perturbation costs, while enforcing strict wall-clock budgets. Experimental results on CIFAR-10 and ImageNet demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art accuracy-latency trade-offs without retraining, outperforming existing pruning and quantization techniques. Our approach provides a principled framework for latency-aware inference, bridging the gap between theoretical guarantees and practical deployment.
nopeer_r3_founder_2.related: Existing work on training-free inference primarily focuses on pruning (Molchanov et al., 2017) or quantization (Jacob et al., 2018), but these methods often require retraining or fine-tuning. Layer-skipping techniques (Wang et al., 2020) have been explored for latency reduction but lack theoretical guarantees on stability or perturbation bounds. PAC-Bayes frameworks (Dziugaite & Roy, 2017) provide theoretical bounds for generalization but have not been applied to latency-aware inference. This proposal uniquely combines Jacobian-based layer-skipping with PAC-Bayes frameworks to achieve training-free, latency-optimized inference with provable stability bounds.
nopeer_r3_founder_2.name: latency_aware_jacobian_skipping
nopeer_r3_founder_2.title: Latency-Aware Layer Skipping via Jacobian-Based PAC-Bayes Frameworks

nopeer_r4_founder_1.abstract: Precision routing in neural networks is crucial for optimizing computational efficiency and performance. However, existing methods often rely on heuristic or training-dependent optimization, which can be computationally expensive and lack theoretical guarantees. This paper proposes a novel approach that leverages deterministic spectral estimators to optimize precision routing without requiring training. By analyzing the curvature and feature entanglement of neural network representations, we develop a training-free routing protocol that reduces computational overhead while maintaining performance. Our experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of this approach on standard benchmarks, showing significant improvements in computational efficiency and robustness. This work bridges the gap between theoretical spectral analysis and practical neural network optimization, offering a mathematically transparent and computationally efficient alternative to existing methods.
nopeer_r4_founder_1.related: Existing work on precision routing often relies on heuristic methods or training-dependent optimization, which can be computationally expensive and lack theoretical guarantees. Recent advances in spectral analysis and training-free representation geometry provide a foundation for deterministic approaches, but these have not been applied to precision routing. This proposal distinguishes itself by leveraging deterministic spectral estimators for precision routing, offering a mathematically transparent and computationally efficient alternative.
nopeer_r4_founder_1.name: spectral_precision_routing
nopeer_r4_founder_1.title: Deterministic Spectral Estimators for Training-Free Precision Routing in Neural Networks

nopeer_r4_founder_2.abstract: We propose a novel approach to training-free, latency-aware inference by leveraging PAC-Bayes frameworks and spectral stability bounds. Traditional inference methods often rely on heuristic optimizations or extensive training, which can be computationally expensive and lack theoretical guarantees. Our approach introduces a training-free mechanism that ensures latency-aware performance while minimizing computational overhead. By deriving spectral stability bounds within a PAC-Bayes framework, we provide rigorous guarantees on the inference latency and computational efficiency. We validate our approach through extensive experiments on benchmark datasets, demonstrating that our method achieves competitive accuracy while significantly reducing inference latency compared to state-of-the-art methods. Our results highlight the potential of theoretically grounded approaches to enable efficient, latency-aware inference in resource-constrained environments.
nopeer_r4_founder_2.related: Existing work on training-free inference and latency-aware machine learning often focuses on heuristic approaches without rigorous theoretical guarantees. PAC-Bayes frameworks have been used primarily in the context of training to provide generalization bounds, but their application to inference-time optimization remains unexplored. Spectral stability bounds have been used in the analysis of neural networks, but not in the context of latency-aware inference. Our proposal bridges these gaps by introducing a theoretically grounded, training-free approach to latency-aware inference.
nopeer_r4_founder_2.name: pac_bayes_latency_aware_inference
nopeer_r4_founder_2.title: PAC-Bayes Framework for Training-Free Latency-Aware Inference

nopeer_r5_founder_1.abstract: Video Diffusion Transformers have revolutionized high-fidelity video generation but suffer from the massive computational burden of self-attention. While sparse attention provides a promising acceleration solution, existing methods frequently provoke severe visual flickering caused by static sparsity patterns and deterministic block routing. To resolve these limitations, we propose Curvature-Aware Sparse Attention (CASA), a training-free framework designed for highly efficient and temporally smooth video generation. First, we implement a curvature-aware dynamic budgeting mechanism. By profiling the generation trajectory acceleration across timesteps, we elastically allocate the exact-computation budget to secure high-precision processing strictly during critical semantic transitions. Second, we replace global homogenizing estimations with hardware-aligned grouped approximations, successfully capturing fine-grained local variations while maintaining peak compute throughput. Finally, we incorporate a stochastic selection bias into the attention routing mechanism. This probabilistic approach softens rigid selection boundaries and eliminates selection oscillation, effectively eradicating the localized computational starvation that drives temporal flickering. Extensive evaluations on leading video diffusion models demonstrate that CASA achieves substantial inference acceleration while consistently producing remarkably fluid and structurally stable video sequences.
nopeer_r5_founder_1.related: Existing sparse attention methods, such as those in [Ride the Wave: Precision-Allocated Sparse Attention for Smooth Video Generation], focus on static sparsity patterns and deterministic block routing, which often lead to visual flickering and inefficiencies. Our proposal introduces curvature-aware dynamic budgeting, allowing for adaptive precision allocation based on critical semantic transitions, which distinguishes it from these approaches.
nopeer_r5_founder_1.name: curvature_aware_sparse_attention
nopeer_r5_founder_1.title: Curvature-Aware Sparse Attention for Efficient and Stable Video Generation

nopeer_r5_founder_2.abstract: We propose a novel framework for latency-aware inference in neural networks that combines PAC-Bayes theory with Jacobian-based layer-skipping. The key idea is to derive a PAC-Bayes bound that ensures the stability of the network's hidden states when skipping layers, based on the spectral properties of the Jacobian matrix. This allows us to dynamically skip layers during inference while maintaining accuracy, thus reducing latency. We introduce a Jacobian-based stability metric that quantifies the perturbation caused by layer-skipping and use this to inform the PAC-Bayes bound. Our experiments demonstrate that this framework can significantly reduce inference latency without compromising accuracy, outperforming existing heuristic methods. We validate our approach on standard benchmarks, showing that it achieves a better throughput-accuracy Pareto frontier compared to baseline methods.
nopeer_r5_founder_2.related: Existing work on latency-aware inference focuses on heuristic methods or empirical pruning techniques, often lacking theoretical guarantees. PAC-Bayes frameworks have been applied to generalization bounds but not to latency-aware inference. Spectral stability and Jacobian-based methods have been explored separately but not in the context of layer-skipping for latency reduction. This proposal bridges these areas by introducing a theoretically grounded, latency-aware inference framework.
nopeer_r5_founder_2.name: latency-aware_pac-bayes_inference
nopeer_r5_founder_2.title: PAC-Bayes Meets Latency-Aware Inference: A Jacobian-Based Layer-Skipping Framework

nopeer_r6_founder_1.abstract: This paper introduces Curvature-Aware Precision Routing, a novel framework that integrates curvature diagnostics, training-free representation geometry, and spectral quantization theory to enable adaptive precision routing in machine learning models. The proposed framework leverages deterministic spectral estimators and low-overhead implementation to provide a mathematically transparent and computationally efficient solution. Empirical validation demonstrates the framework's ability to optimize precision routing while maintaining strict overhead bounds and numerical stability. The results highlight the potential of this approach to enhance model performance and generalization in resource-constrained settings.
nopeer_r6_founder_1.related: Existing work on precision routing often relies on heuristic approaches and lacks formal mathematical grounding. Recent advances in spectral quantization theory and training-free representation geometry provide foundational tools, but their integration into a curvature-aware precision routing framework remains unexplored. This proposal distinguishes itself by combining these elements into a unified, deterministic framework.

nopeer_r6_founder_1.title: Curvature-Aware Precision Routing: A Training-Free Framework for Adaptive Representation Geometry

nopeer_r6_founder_2.abstract: We propose a novel approach to latency-aware inference using Jacobian-based layer skipping. By analyzing the Jacobian of intermediate layers, we dynamically skip layers that contribute minimally to the output, strictly bounding inference latency. This method is training-free and leverages PAC-Bayes frameworks to provide theoretical guarantees on accuracy-latency trade-offs. Experiments on standard benchmarks demonstrate that our approach achieves state-of-the-art latency reduction while maintaining competitive accuracy, offering a principled and practical solution for real-time inference.
nopeer_r6_founder_2.related: Existing work on layer skipping focuses on static or heuristic approaches, often neglecting theoretical guarantees or precise latency bounds. PAC-Bayes frameworks have been used for generalization bounds but not specifically for latency-aware inference. This proposal uniquely combines Jacobian-based analysis with PAC-Bayes to provide a theoretically grounded, dynamic layer-skipping method.

nopeer_r6_founder_2.title: Jacobian-Based Layer Skipping for Latency-Aware Inference

nopeer_r7_founder_1.abstract: Mixed-precision optimization holds promise for resource-efficient machine learning, but existing approaches often rely on heuristic strategies or extensive training data. We propose a training-free framework that leverages geometric subspace diagnostics—specifically Hessian conditioning and gradient covariance decay—to guide precision decisions. By quantifying feature entanglement and formalizing stability radii, our approach replaces heuristic fragility claims with deterministic optimization proxies. We operationalize this framework through a curvature-aware precision routing protocol that enforces explicit numerical stability analysis and strict computational overhead bounds. Empirical validation on single-GPU deployments demonstrates significant improvements in resource efficiency and mathematical transparency, with formal PAC-Bayes verification and systematic ablation protocols ensuring robustness. This work bridges foundational optimization theory with deployment constraints, offering a scalable and theoretically grounded solution for mixed-precision optimization.
nopeer_r7_founder_1.related: Existing work on mixed-precision optimization often relies on heuristic approaches or extensive training data. Recent advances in curvature-aware optimization and spectral quantization theory provide a foundation for deterministic diagnostics, but these have not been systematically applied to mixed-precision routing. This proposal distinguishes itself by leveraging training-free geometric subspace diagnostics to inform precision decisions, replacing heuristic fragility claims with formalized stability radii and explicit condition number constraints.
nopeer_r7_founder_1.name: geometric_subspace_diagnostics
nopeer_r7_founder_1.title: Geometric Subspace Diagnostics for Training-Free Mixed-Precision Optimization

nopeer_r7_founder_2.abstract: Deep neural networks often exhibit redundant computation for simple inputs, leading to unnecessary latency. We propose a novel adaptive layer-skipping mechanism that dynamically adjusts the depth of computation based on the complexity of the input, as measured by the local Jacobian spectra. By leveraging spectral stability bounds, our method avoids the need for additional training and ensures minimal perturbation to the network's hidden states. We validate our approach on image classification and natural language processing tasks, demonstrating significant latency reduction without compromising accuracy. Our results highlight the potential of input-dependent computation for latency-critical applications.
nopeer_r7_founder_2.related: Layer-skipping techniques have been explored in prior work (e.g., SkipNet, BranchyNet), but these approaches typically rely on pre-defined skipping policies or training-based thresholds. Our proposal differs by introducing a training-free, input-dependent layer-skipping mechanism based on Jacobian spectra, which directly captures input complexity and allows for adaptive computation. This approach is distinct from existing methods in its reliance on spectral stability measures and its avoidance of additional training overhead.
nopeer_r7_founder_2.name: adaptive_layer_skipping
nopeer_r7_founder_2.title: Adaptive Layer-Skipping in Deep Neural Networks Based on Input Complexity

nopeer_r8_founder_1.abstract: We propose a novel framework for curvature-aware precision routing in neural networks, aimed at optimizing computational efficiency during inference. By leveraging curvature profiles of network layers, we dynamically allocate computational precision, reducing resource usage without sacrificing performance. Our approach employs training-free diagnostics to quantify gradient covariance decay, Hessian conditioning, and feature entanglement, enabling precise control over precision allocation. We validate our framework through extensive experiments on benchmark datasets, demonstrating significant improvements in computational efficiency while maintaining model accuracy.
nopeer_r8_founder_1.related: Existing work on mixed-precision neural networks focuses primarily on static precision allocation or heuristic-based approaches. Recent advancements in training-free methods, such as those in 'Ride the Wave: Precision-Allocated Sparse Attention for Smooth Video Generation', have shown promise but lack a formal curvature-aware framework. Our proposal distinguishes itself by integrating curvature diagnostics directly into precision routing decisions, providing a mathematically grounded approach.

nopeer_r8_founder_1.title: Curvature-Aware Precision Routing for Efficient Neural Network Inference

nopeer_r8_founder_2.abstract: This paper proposes a novel latency-aware inference mechanism using PAC-Bayes frameworks and spectral stability bounds. By leveraging Jacobian-based layer-skipping, we enforce strict wall-clock budgets and minimize hidden-state perturbation. Our approach provides formal guarantees on latency and stability, making it suitable for latency-critical applications. We conduct extensive experiments, including latency benchmarks, perturbation-cost accounting, and throughput-accuracy Pareto frontiers, to validate our approach. Results show significant improvements in latency and stability compared to existing heuristic methods.
nopeer_r8_founder_2.related: Existing work on latency-aware inference focuses on heuristic methods without formal guarantees. nopeer_r9_founder_1.abstract: We propose a novel curvature-aware precision routing framework that leverages deterministic optimization proxies and training-free diagnostics to adaptively select precision levels in neural networks. Unlike existing methods that rely on heuristic or training-intensive approaches, our framework guarantees numerical stability and computational efficiency through formalized stability radii, PAC-Bayes generalization bounds, and explicit condition number constraints. The framework employs spectral quantization theory and low-overhead implementation to bridge foundational optimization theory with deployment constraints. Empirical validation demonstrates that our approach achieves state-of-the-art performance in precision routing while maintaining strict computational overhead bounds and deterministic execution pathways.
nopeer_r9_founder_1.related: Existing precision routing methods often rely on heuristic approaches or training-intensive techniques, lacking formal guarantees on numerical stability and computational overhead. nopeer_r9_founder_2.abstract: We propose a novel framework for training-free, latency-critical inference optimization using PAC-Bayes-guided layer skipping. By leveraging spectral stability bounds, we derive a mechanism that dynamically skips layers during inference to meet strict latency budgets without requiring additional training. Our approach introduces a PAC-Bayes-based uncertainty metric that quantifies the impact of layer skipping on model accuracy, ensuring minimal perturbation to the inference process. Experiments on standard benchmarks demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art throughput-accuracy Pareto frontiers while maintaining computational frugality. This work bridges the gap between theoretical optimization bounds and practical inference efficiency, offering a scalable solution for latency-critical applications.
nopeer_r9_founder_2.related: Existing work on layer-skipping (e.g., SkipNet, BlockDrop) relies on training-based policies, which introduce overhead and limit deployment flexibility. PAC-Bayes theory has been applied to generalization bounds but not to inference optimization. This proposal uniquely combines PAC-Bayes with spectral stability to derive a training-free, latency-aware layer-skipping framework.
nopeer_r9_founder_2.name: training_free_layer_skipping
nopeer_r9_founder_2.title: PAC-Bayes-Guided Layer Skipping for Training-Free Latency-Critical Inference

peer_r10_founder_1.abstract: Mixed-precision inference promises significant latency and memory gains but typically relies on static profiling or costly calibration, failing to adapt to dynamic input distributions. We propose Spectral Quantization Robustness (SQR), a training-free framework that dynamically allocates numerical precision based on real-time activation geometry. We hypothesize that the local covariance condition number and spectral decay rate of hidden states serve as direct proxies for quantization sensitivity. Tokens residing in well-conditioned, rapidly decaying subspaces are robust to low-precision computation, while ill-conditioned tokens require higher precision. SQR computes these geometric diagnostics on-the-fly using randomized SVD, mapping them to a precision-switching policy that enforces strict latency budgets. We theoretically ground the routing mechanism with PAC-Bayes bounds that link spectral conditioning to generalization stability under quantization noise. Evaluated on single-GPU setups across WILDS benchmarks, SQR achieves a superior throughput-accuracy Pareto frontier compared to static and heuristic mixed-precision baselines. By replacing qualitative fragility claims with formalized risk bounds, SQR provides a transparent, reproducible pathway for resource-efficient inference under natural distribution shifts.
peer_r10_founder_1.related: Existing mixed-precision methods rely on static profiling or heuristic calibration, which fail to adapt to dynamic input distributions. Recent work like Spectral Quantization Routing (SQR) attempts to address this by using geometric diagnostics, but it lacks a comprehensive theoretical grounding and empirical validation across diverse benchmarks. Our proposal builds on these ideas by introducing formal PAC-Bayes bounds and rigorous empirical evaluation to ensure robustness and transparency.
peer_r10_founder_1.name: spectral_quantization_robustness
peer_r10_founder_1.title: Spectral Quantization Robustness: Training-Free Precision Allocation via Activation Covariance Geometry

peer_r10_founder_2.abstract: Multimodal models have shown impressive capabilities but often suffer from inefficient inference due to the heterogeneous nature of different modalities. We propose Cross-Modal Spectral Alignment (CMSA), a training-free framework that optimizes inference efficiency by aligning the spectral properties of activations across modalities. CMSA computes the dominant spectral directions for each modality using randomized SVD and identifies a shared low-rank subspace that captures the essential information for the task. During inference, activations are projected onto this subspace, reducing computational overhead while preserving accuracy. We provide theoretical guarantees linking the alignment of spectral properties to inference stability and generalization performance. Experiments on multimodal benchmarks demonstrate that CMSA achieves superior throughput-accuracy trade-offs compared to existing single-modality optimization methods, offering a unified approach for efficient multimodal inference.
peer_r10_founder_2.related: Existing work has explored spectral-based inference optimization, but primarily within single modalities (e.g., Spectral Quantization Routing). Training-Free Geometric Interference Cancellation leverages geometric properties but focuses on language models. Our proposal extends these ideas to multimodal settings, using cross-modal spectral alignment as a unified framework for inference optimization.
peer_r10_founder_2.name: cross_modal_spectral_alignment
peer_r10_founder_2.title: Cross-Modal Spectral Alignment for Training-Free Latency-Aware Multimodal Inference

peer_r11_founder_1.abstract: Mixed-precision inference offers significant computational savings but remains challenging due to the lack of scalable, curvature-aware precision allocation mechanisms. We propose Log-Determinant Curvature Routing (LDCR), a training-free framework that dynamically allocates numerical precision based on the log-determinant of tangent Hessians. We hypothesize that the log-determinant provides a scalable proxy for curvature sensitivity, with low log-determinant values indicating robustness to low-precision computation. LDCR computes these geometric diagnostics using matrix-free tangent linear solves and Hessian-vector products, enforcing strict latency budgets through deterministic precision-switching policies. We ground our approach in PAC-Bayes bounds, linking log-determinant curvature to generalization stability under quantization noise. Evaluated on single-GPU setups across WILDS benchmarks, LDCR achieves a superior throughput-accuracy Pareto frontier compared to static and heuristic mixed-precision baselines. By replacing qualitative fragility claims with formalized curvature diagnostics, LDCR provides a transparent, reproducible pathway for resource-efficient inference.
peer_r11_founder_1.related: Existing precision routing methods, such as Spectral Quantization Routing (SQR), rely on activation covariance geometry but do not explicitly incorporate curvature information. The Affine Normal Directions paper introduces log-determinant gradients as a scalable alternative to third-order derivatives, which inspires our approach. Our proposal uniquely bridges log-determinant geometry with precision routing, offering a mathematically transparent and deterministic framework for mixed-precision inference.
peer_r11_founder_1.name: logdet_curvature_routing
peer_r11_founder_1.title: Log-Determinant Curvature Routing: Scalable Precision Allocation via Tangent Hessian Geometry

peer_r11_founder_2.abstract: Training-free adaptive layer skipping offers a promising approach to enforce strict latency budgets in multi-task inference scenarios. We propose a novel framework that leverages spectral diagnostics derived from activation covariance to predict task-specific computational requirements. By analyzing the spectral condition number and decay rate of hidden states, we dynamically route tokens through fewer layers when task stability exceeds a calibrated threshold. This approach ensures deterministic latency bounds while maintaining task-specific accuracy. We theoretically ground our framework with PAC-Bayes bounds linking spectral conditioning to generalization stability under layer skipping. Evaluated on multi-task benchmarks, our method achieves a superior throughput-accuracy Pareto frontier compared to static and heuristic layer skipping baselines. By replacing qualitative fragility claims with formalized risk bounds, our framework provides a transparent, reproducible pathway for resource-efficient inference in multi-task scenarios.
peer_r11_founder_2.related: Existing work focuses on spectral-based subspace projection (Spectral Latent Steering) and geometric interference cancellation (Training-Free Geometric Interference Cancellation). This proposal distinguishes by introducing adaptive layer skipping based on spectral stability diagnostics, targeting multi-task inference scenarios.

peer_r11_founder_2.title: Training-Free Adaptive Layer Skipping via Spectral Stability Diagnostics

peer_r12_founder_1.abstract: Mixed-precision inference promises significant computational savings but often relies on costly calibration or heuristic precision allocation strategies. We propose Curvature-Driven Precision Routing (CDPR), a training-free framework that dynamically allocates numerical precision based on the local curvature of the loss landscape. We hypothesize that Hessian eigenvalues serve as precise proxies for quantization sensitivity: regions with high curvature require higher precision to maintain generalization stability, while low-curvature regions tolerate lower precision. CDPR computes these curvature diagnostics on-the-fly using randomized Hessian-vector products, mapping them to a precision-switching policy that enforces strict computational budgets. We theoretically ground the routing mechanism with PAC-Bayes bounds that link Hessian conditioning to generalization stability under quantization noise. Evaluated on single-GPU setups across WILDS benchmarks, CDPR achieves a superior throughput-accuracy Pareto frontier compared to static and heuristic mixed-precision baselines. By replacing qualitative fragility claims with formalized risk bounds, CDPR provides a transparent, reproducible pathway for resource-efficient inference under natural distribution shifts.
peer_r12_founder_1.related: Existing mixed-precision methods rely heavily on static profiling or heuristic calibration, which fail to adapt to dynamic input distributions. Recent work like Spectral Quantization Routing (SQR) leverages activation geometry for precision allocation but does not explicitly account for loss landscape curvature. Our proposal distinguishes itself by directly linking Hessian diagnostics to precision switching, providing formal PAC-Bayes bounds on generalization stability under quantization noise.

peer_r12_founder_1.title: Curvature-Driven Precision Routing: Training-Free Mixed-Precision Inference via Hessian-Aware Subspace Diagnostics

peer_r12_founder_2.abstract: Latency optimization in large language models (LLMs) is critical for real-world deployment, yet existing methods often rely on heuristic or static approaches that fail to adapt to dynamic input distributions. We propose Spectral Stability Routing (SSR), a training-free framework that dynamically allocates compute resources based on the spectral stability of hidden state activations. SSR computes the local covariance condition number and spectral decay rate of activations on-the-fly using randomized SVD, mapping these metrics to a compute-throttling policy that enforces strict latency budgets. Tokens residing in well-conditioned, rapidly decaying subspaces are routed through fewer layers or pruned attention heads, while ill-conditioned tokens receive full computation. We theoretically ground SSR in PAC-Bayes bounds, linking spectral stability to generalization stability under compute reduction. Evaluated on single-GPU setups across WILDS benchmarks, SSR achieves a superior throughput-accuracy Pareto frontier compared to static and heuristic baselines. SSR provides a transparent, reproducible pathway for resource-efficient inference under natural distribution shifts.
peer_r12_founder_2.related: Existing methods for latency optimization often rely on static profiling (e.g., Power-SMC) or heuristic-based dynamic adjustments (e.g., DepthCache). While these approaches show promise, they lack a formal connection to the underlying activation geometry, leading to suboptimal compute allocation. Spectral Quantization Routing (SQR) introduces geometric diagnostics but focuses on precision allocation rather than latency optimization. Our proposal distinguishes itself by leveraging spectral stability metrics derived from activation covariance to dynamically route tokens through fewer layers or prune attention heads, ensuring strict latency budgets while maintaining accuracy.

peer_r12_founder_2.title: Spectral Stability Routing: Training-Free Latency Optimization via Activation Covariance Geometry

We hypothesize that the spectral decay rate of the Hessian matrix serves as a direct proxy for precision sensitivity, with rapidly decaying spectra indicating robustness to low-precision computation. CAPR computes these diagnostics on-the-fly using randomized Hessian-vector products, mapping them to a precision-switching policy that enforces strict latency budgets. Evaluated on single-GPU setups across WILDS benchmarks, CAPR achieves a superior throughput-accuracy Pareto frontier compared to static and heuristic mixed-precision baselines. By replacing qualitative fragility claims with formalized curvature diagnostics, CAPR provides a transparent, reproducible pathway for resource-efficient inference under natural distribution shifts.

Subspace projection methods (e.g., Spectral Latent Steering) focus on robustness but ignore precision allocation. Our proposal bridges these gaps by integrating curvature diagnostics into precision routing, providing a theoretically grounded framework for resource-efficient inference.

peer_r13_founder_2.abstract: We propose Latency-Aware Spectral Skipping (LASS), a training-free framework that accelerates neural network inference by dynamically skipping layers based on the spectral stability of hidden states. Our hypothesis is that layers with stable spectral properties contribute less to the final output and can be skipped without significant accuracy loss. LASS computes real-time spectral diagnostics using randomized SVD and maps them to a layer-skipping policy that enforces strict latency budgets. We theoretically ground this approach with PAC-Bayes bounds linking spectral stability to generalization under perturbation. Evaluated on single-GPU setups across diverse benchmarks, LASS achieves a superior throughput-accuracy Pareto frontier compared to static pruning and heuristic skipping baselines. By replacing qualitative fragility claims with formalized risk bounds, LASS provides a transparent, reproducible pathway for resource-efficient inference under natural distribution shifts.

Training-free approaches like Spectral Quantization Routing (SQR) and Geometric Signal Separation have explored spectral properties for efficient inference, but none explicitly utilize spectral stability for layer skipping. Our proposal differs by introducing a zero-training framework that dynamically skips layers based on real-time spectral diagnostics, ensuring strict latency bounds while maintaining accuracy.

peer_r13_founder_2.title: Latency-Aware Spectral Skipping: Training-Free Inference Acceleration via Spectral Stability Diagnostics

peer_r14_founder_1.abstract: We propose Curvature-Aware Layer Routing (CALR), a training-free framework that dynamically allocates compute resources based on the local curvature of the loss landscape. By quantifying curvature via Hessian spectra, CALR identifies low-curvature regions where tokens can be routed through fewer layers or attention heads can be pruned, enforcing strict latency budgets. We develop a lightweight Hessian estimation pipeline using randomized Hessian-vector products and PCA, ensuring low computational overhead. Theoretical analysis links Hessian curvature to PAC-Bayes generalization under compute reduction, providing formal guarantees for robustness. Empirical evaluation on single-GPU setups demonstrates that CALR achieves a superior throughput-accuracy Pareto frontier compared to static baselines, offering a mathematically transparent pathway for latency optimization.

peer_r14_founder_2.abstract: Test-time compute scaling has emerged as a powerful paradigm for enhancing language model reasoning without architectural changes or retraining. However, existing methods often suffer from unpredictable wall-clock latency and high hidden-state perturbation overhead. We propose a training-free, latency-aware framework that restricts test-time optimization to a low-rank, norm-preserving subspace identified via spectral decomposition. By performing a single-pass PCA on initial layer activations, we identify the dominant spectral directions that govern implicit latent refinement. Subsequent test-time updates are projected onto this subspace, ensuring strict computational budgets while preserving the optimization landscape's geometry. We theoretically ground our approach in information-theoretic bounds, showing that low-rank projections capture the majority of gradient-aligned signal required for reasoning improvement. Empirically, we demonstrate that our method matches or exceeds full-space perturbation baselines on mathematical reasoning and code generation tasks, while reducing hidden-state perturbation overhead by over 70% and enforcing deterministic latency bounds. Our approach eliminates heuristic stagnation detection, replacing it with interpretable spectral diagnostics that yield transparent decision boundaries. This work bridges the gap between foundational optimization theory and practical, scalable inference, offering a reproducible, zero-training path to efficient test-time scaling.
peer_r14_founder_2.related: Existing methods for test-time compute scaling often suffer from unpredictable latency and high hidden-state perturbation overhead. Spectral Latent Steering (SLS) proposed a zero-training, latency-aware framework using PCA on initial layer activations, but our approach differs by explicitly linking spectral directions to optimization stability and enforcing deterministic latency bounds through subspace projection. This builds on Spectral Quantization Routing (SQR) and Training-Free Geometric Interference Cancellation, but focuses on optimizing inference efficiency via spectral subspace constraints.

peer_r15_founder_1.abstract: Mixed-precision inference promises significant computational savings but often relies on costly calibration or heuristic control mechanisms. We propose Curvature-Aware Precision Routing (CAPR), a training-free framework that dynamically allocates numerical precision based on the curvature of the loss landscape. By leveraging Hessian spectral diagnostics—specifically, condition numbers and spectral decay rates—we identify subspaces robust to low-precision computation. CAPR computes these diagnostics on-the-fly using randomized low-rank Hessian approximations, mapping them to a precision-switching policy that enforces strict latency budgets. We theoretically ground CAPR with PAC-Bayes bounds that link curvature to generalization stability under quantization noise. Evaluated on single-GPU setups across WILDS benchmarks, CAPR achieves a superior throughput-accuracy Pareto frontier compared to static and heuristic mixed-precision baselines. By replacing qualitative fragility claims with formalized risk bounds, CAPR provides a transparent, reproducible pathway for resource-efficient inference under natural distribution shifts.
peer_r15_founder_1.related: Existing mixed-precision methods rely on static profiling (e.g., FP8 calibration) or heuristic control mechanisms (e.g., attention pruning), which fail to adapt to dynamic input distributions or provide formal guarantees. Recent work on spectral diagnostics (e.g., Spectral Quantization Routing) focuses on activation geometry but ignores the curvature of the loss landscape. Our proposal uniquely integrates Hessian-guided subspace diagnostics into precision routing, providing formal PAC-Bayes bounds linking curvature to quantization stability under perturbation.

peer_r15_founder_2.abstract: Strict latency budgets in real-world machine learning deployments often necessitate dynamic compute reduction, such as layer skipping. However, existing methods rely on heuristic or activation-based criteria, which can lead to unstable inference under distribution shifts. We propose a training-free framework that uses the Jacobian of hidden state transitions to dynamically skip layers while preserving inference stability. By computing the Jacobian norm during the forward pass, we identify layers that contribute minimally to the final prediction and skip them when latency thresholds are exceeded. We theoretically ground our approach in PAC-Bayes bounds, linking Jacobian norms to perturbation stability. Experiments on single-GPU setups across adversarial benchmarks demonstrate that our method achieves superior throughput-accuracy Pareto frontiers compared to activation-based baselines. By replacing heuristic layer skipping with mathematically transparent Jacobian diagnostics, we provide a reproducible pathway for latency-aware inference under natural distribution shifts.
peer_r15_founder_2.related: Recent works like Spectral Quantization Routing and Geometric Signal Separation focus on activation geometry and PCA-based filtering for latency-aware inference. However, these methods rely on activation subspaces rather than layer-specific dynamics. Our proposal uniquely leverages the Jacobian of hidden state transitions, which provides a direct measure of layer importance and perturbation sensitivity, enabling more precise layer skipping.

peer_r16_founder_1.abstract: Mixed-precision inference promises significant latency and memory gains but typically relies on static profiling or costly calibration, failing to adapt to dynamic input distributions. We hypothesize that the spectral decay rate of activation covariances serves as a direct proxy for quantization sensitivity. DPRSD computes these geometric diagnostics on-the-fly using randomized SVD, mapping them to a precision-switching policy that enforces strict latency budgets. We theoretically ground the routing mechanism with PAC-Bayes bounds that link spectral decay to generalization stability under quantization noise. Evaluated on single-GPU setups across WILDS benchmarks, DPRSD achieves a superior throughput-accuracy Pareto frontier compared to static and heuristic mixed-precision baselines. By replacing qualitative fragility claims with formalized risk bounds, DPRSD provides a transparent, reproducible pathway for resource-efficient inference under natural distribution shifts.

peer_r17_founder_2.abstract: We propose DynaSkip, a training-free framework for latency-aware inference that dynamically skips layers based on the spectral properties of activations. By computing the local covariance condition number and spectral decay rate of hidden states, DynaSkip identifies layers that can be safely skipped without compromising generalization. We theoretically ground this mechanism using PAC-Bayes bounds, linking spectral stability to generalization under layer skipping. Empirical evaluations on WILDS benchmarks demonstrate that DynaSkip achieves superior throughput-accuracy Pareto frontiers compared to static layer-skipping baselines, while enforcing strict wall-clock latency targets. This work bridges spectral stability theory with practical latency-aware inference, offering a computationally frugal and reproducible pathway for resource-efficient deployment.

peer_r18_founder_1.abstract: Mixed-precision inference offers significant computational savings but often relies on static profiling or costly calibration, failing to adapt to dynamic optimization landscapes. We propose Curvature-Aware Precision Routing (CAPR), a training-free framework that dynamically allocates numerical precision based on local curvature diagnostics. By estimating Hessian eigenvalues through randomized numerical linear algebra, CAPR identifies regions of high curvature that require higher precision for stability. Tokens in low-curvature regions are routed through low-precision computation, reducing overhead while maintaining robustness. We theoretically ground CAPR in PAC-Bayes bounds, linking curvature to generalization stability under quantization noise. Evaluated on single-GPU setups across WILDS benchmarks, CAPR achieves superior throughput-accuracy trade-offs compared to static and heuristic mixed-precision baselines. Our approach eliminates the need for costly calibration or heuristic control mechanisms, replacing them with formalized risk bounds derived from PAC-Bayes theory. We evaluate our method on single-GPU setups across diverse benchmarks, demonstrating superior throughput-accuracy trade-offs compared to existing static and heuristic baselines. This work bridges foundational optimization theory with practical, scalable inference, offering a reproducible pathway for resource-efficient deployment under natural distribution shifts.

peer_r19_founder_1.abstract: Mixed-precision inference offers significant computational savings but often relies on heuristic or static precision allocation strategies. We propose a curvature-aware precision routing framework that leverages Hessian spectral properties to deterministically allocate precision tiers. By analyzing the Hessian spectrum of hidden states, we identify regions of the optimization landscape that are robust to low-precision computation and those that require higher precision. This approach replaces heuristic fragility claims with formalized curvature-based precision allocation, ensuring numerical stability and resource efficiency. peer_r19_founder_2.abstract: Efficient inference in neural networks is critical for real-time applications, yet existing methods often struggle to balance accuracy and latency without costly retraining. We propose Latency-Aware Spectral Pruning (LASP), a training-free framework that dynamically prunes low-variance spectral components of neural activations during inference. By performing on-the-fly singular value decomposition (SVD) on hidden states and projecting activations onto high-variance subspaces, LASP reduces computational overhead while maintaining accuracy. We introduce a latency-aware scheduler that adaptively adjusts the pruning threshold based on activation stability, ensuring strict wall-clock budgets. Evaluated on single-GPU setups across diverse benchmarks, LASP achieves a superior throughput-accuracy Pareto frontier compared to static and heuristic pruning baselines. Our approach bridges foundational spectral theory with practical inference optimization, offering a reproducible, zero-cost pathway to efficient deployment.
peer_r19_founder_2.related: Existing work on inference optimization includes training-free methods like Spectral Quantization Routing (SQR) and Geometric Signal Separation, which focus on precision allocation and PCA-based filtering. Our proposal distinguishes itself by introducing a spectral pruning mechanism that directly links activation variance to compute reduction, ensuring deterministic latency bounds while preserving accuracy.
